title: Stichorkis arrigens
taxonomy: Stichorkis arrigens is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. This species belongs to the genus Stichorkis, a group of terrestrial orchids commonly found in tropical regions. Stichorkis arrigens is further categorized as a species of epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants, often in humid environments. The classification of Stichorkis arrigens is also influenced by its geographical distribution, which is primarily limited to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. Further taxonomic analysis would be needed to provide a more detailed classification.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential benefits in treating various health conditions such as wound healing and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stichorkis arrigens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
